title: Taxi Drivers
description: Drive a motor vehicle to transport passengers on an unplanned basis and charge a fare, usually based on a meter.

tasks.text: Collect fares or vouchers from passengers, and make change or issue receipts as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Communicate with dispatchers by radio, telephone, or computer to exchange information and receive requests for passenger service.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine fares based on trip distances and times, using taximeters and fee schedules, and announce fares to passengers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive taxicabs or privately owned vehicles to transport passengers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Follow relevant safety regulations and state laws governing vehicle operation, and ensure that passengers follow safety regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Notify dispatchers or company mechanics of vehicle problems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform minor vehicle repairs, such as cleaning spark plugs, or take vehicles to mechanics for servicing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform routine vehicle maintenance, such as regulating tire pressure and adding gasoline, oil, and water.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Pick up passengers at prearranged locations, at taxi stands, or by cruising streets in high-traffic areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide passengers with assistance entering and exiting vehicles, and help them with any luggage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide passengers with information or advice about the local area, points of interest, hotels, or restaurants.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Report to taxicab services or garages to receive vehicle assignments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test vehicle equipment, such as lights, brakes, horns, or windshield wipers, to ensure proper operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Turn the taximeter on when passengers enter the cab, and turn it off when they reach the final destination.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Vacuum and clean interiors and wash and polish exteriors of automobiles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
